title: Fix admin principal auth: correct actor identity wiring and backend isAdmin check

requirements.text: Fix frontend actor instantiation in useActor.ts so that after Internet Identity authentication succeeds, the ICP actor is always re-created using the authenticated identity object (not the anonymous identity). The actor instance must be invalidated and re-created whenever the identity transitions from anonymous to authenticated. All mutation calls in useQueries.ts must use this authenticated actor so the backend receives the correct caller principal.

quotes: Option 1 fixes the frontend: makes sure the ICP actor is created with the authenticated II identity, not the default anonymous one
fix the frontend actor instantiation in useActor.ts to ensure that when a user is authenticated via Internet Identity, the actor is always created with the authenticated identity

acceptanceCriteria: After II login on /admin, the actor held in React Query cache is created with the authenticated identity, not the anonymous identity
The actor is re-created and dependent query caches are invalidated when identity changes from anonymous to authenticated

All CMS mutation calls (updateAbout, updateFeatures, updateGameDetails, updateInstagram, updateDeveloperWebsite, updatePressEmail, updateBodyTextColor, enablePasswordProtection, disablePasswordProtection) use the authenticated actor
No anonymous-principal actor is used for any admin update call after login

requirements.text: Fix the backend isAdmin authorization logic in backend/main.mo so that update methods correctly accept the authenticated admin principal. Audit the isAdmin() / caller verification function: ensure the stored adminPrincipal Option value is properly unwrapped before comparison, that no code path falls through to an anonymous-principal false-positive rejection, and that every admin-only update method (updateAbout, updateFeatures, updateGameDetails, updateInstagram, updateDeveloperWebsite, updatePressEmail, updateBodyTextColor, enablePasswordProtection, disablePasswordProtection) uses this corrected check.

quotes: Option 2 fixes the backend: makes sure the stored adminPrincipal (which is an ?Principal option type in Motoko) is unwrapped correctly before comparing to the caller
if adminPrincipal is stored as ?Principal (an Option), there could be an unwrapping bug where it always resolves to anonymous or null

acceptanceCriteria: isAdmin() correctly unwraps the ?Principal option and compares it to msg.caller without any false-positive anonymous rejections
Calling updateAbout (and all other admin update methods) with the registered admin principal returns #ok and persists the change
Calling any admin update method with a non-admin principal still returns an access-denied error
initializeAdmin() still works correctly: first caller sets the admin, subsequent calls from the same principal return #ok, different principal returns access-denied error

constraints: Do not introduce any token-based or link-based authentication mechanism
Do not modify files under frontend/src/hooks/useInternetIdentity.ts or frontend/src/main.tsx
Keep all Motoko logic within the single backend/main.mo actor
Do not change the existing backend stable variable schema or CMS API surface
nonGoals: Adding any new CMS fields or admin UI features
Changing the Internet Identity login flow or UI
Adding a getCurrentAdmin() debug query to the public API
